title: Bootstrap lockfile tooling in release workflow
rawUserRequest: 发布被流程或过时代码阻挡时先修复流程再继续；监控 GitHub Actions，完成 v0.5.39 发布。

declaredIntent.summary: Make the release workflow install its pinned lockfile compiler dependency before invoking the reproducibility gate, with executable ordering coverage.

outOfScope: Retrying release run 30098390871 or creating v0.5.39 tag/Release in this Work Item
Changing dependency versions beyond matching the already validated compatibility workflow bootstrap
Editing release metadata before this workflow fix is merged and closed
Weakening lockfile reproducibility or exact-source release checks

sources.reason: Hosted failure: /usr/bin/python3: No module named piptools before any tag mutation.
Release workflow installs requirements-dev.lock but omits pip-tools before lockfile reproducibility.
Working hosted precedent installs pip-tools==7.6.0 and typing-extensions==4.16.0 before the same Make target.
check-lockfile-reproducibility invokes the selected interpreter with -m piptools compile.
Existing workflow contract tests lack release-specific install-before-check ordering.

riskAssessment.reason: A missing hosted tool dependency blocks publication after expensive source validation; ordering must be executable and fail closed.

executionDecision.reason: The failing run, missing dependency, working compatibility precedent, and safe no-tag state are directly observed.

preReviewWarnings: Prove the release workflow installs pinned pip-tools before lockfile reproducibility.
Do not weaken the reproducibility gate or retry publication here.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A regression test fails before implementation because release.yml does not install pip-tools before check-lockfile-reproducibility.
release.yml installs pip-tools==7.6.0 and typing-extensions==4.16.0 before the reproducibility step, matching the successful compatibility workflow.
Workflow tests assert both the pinned command and its ordering before make check-lockfile-reproducibility.
Distribution documentation states that both compatibility and release runners bootstrap the lockfile tool explicitly.
Focused workflow tests and full quality pass; archiveGrowth 541 remains warning-only.
The corrective PR passes Actions, merges, closes through ai-close-work-item, and exact merged main passes post-merge Actions before release preparation resumes.
guidelines: Fix hosted release prerequisites at the workflow boundary rather than bypassing the gate.
Reuse the pinned tool versions already validated by compatibility CI.
Keep all tag and Release mutation blocked until the correction is merged and closed.
Use TDD and require install-before-check ordering evidence.

intent.problem: Release run 30098390871 passed exact-source identity and archive preflight, then failed because release.yml invoked python3 -m piptools without installing pip-tools. Compatibility CI has the required bootstrap, so release.yml is an obsolete divergent path.
constraints: No tag or GitHub Release exists and none may be created in this corrective Work Item.
Add the smallest workflow bootstrap matching compatibility.yml.
Test command presence and ordering before implementation.
Complete PR, merge, closure, and exact-main Actions before regenerating release metadata.
intent.rationale: The release runner must own every tool prerequisite for its fail-closed gates; reusing the compatibility bootstrap removes environment dependence without weakening reproducibility.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The exact-main release workflow fails before mutation because piptools is absent.
Compatibility CI installs pinned pip-tools and passes the same reproducibility target.
Failure occurs before tag and Draft Release creation.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user authorized process-first workflow corrections and delegated execution.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

budgetImpact.reason: One functional release-workflow correction archive; archive growth remains warning-only.
